proposal: Markets stall on exact change. Vendors refuse bills. Coins have left the country like unpaid interns.

options.label: Admit the coin shortage
options.result_text: Penny posts Form Coin-0. Queues form around empty trays.

options.label: Blame private piggy banks
options.result_text: Elites hide jars. Markets still cannot make change for bread.

options.label: Call it a rounding mood
options.result_text: Vendors invent creative math. Penny refuses to watch.

authoring.visual_hook: empty coin trays at market stalls
authoring.writer_notes: Distinct from tip jar; soft remedy only on shortage paths.

proposal: Your coin shortage still freezes stalls. Penny offers temporary fixes before the ledger invents barter.

options.label: Mandate price rounding up
options.result_text: Every loaf gains a tax of dignity. Coins become optional mythology.

options.label: Issue temporary market tokens
options.result_text: Cardboard coins circulate. Form Token-1 smells like wet ink.

options.label: Mint emergency coins overnight
options.result_text: Soft metal discs appear by dawn. Some still say 'prototype'.

authoring.visual_hook: rounding booths and cardboard tokens
authoring.writer_notes: Explicit callback to coin shortage; three temporary remedies.

proposal: Public clocks disagree by eleven minutes. Mandate Form 12-Time for national synchronization.

options.label: Synchronize every clock
options.result_text: Every clock ticks in unison. Late people lose their excuses — briefly.

options.label: Allow local drift
options.result_text: Villages keep cozy wrong-o'clock. Trains invent creative arrivals.

authoring.visual_hook: identical public clocks nationwide
authoring.writer_notes: REWRITE from standalone; hard follow-up on sync path only.

proposal: Your Form 12-Time sync made every clock agree — and every appointment wrong. Midwives, trains, and bakery shifts arrive together and late.

options.label: Keep rigid national time
options.result_text: Missed buses become civic education. Olga files Complaint Form: Time.

options.label: Allow staggered district zones
options.result_text: Districts get polite disagreements. Trains negotiate arrival.

options.label: Repeal the sync mandate
options.result_text: Clocks drift again. Excuses return with paperwork.

authoring.visual_hook: missed appointment boards under synced clocks
authoring.writer_notes: Civic consequence of sync; cancels if law removed.

proposal: Weekends produce zero invoiceable output. Abolish them and gain two sevenths productivity.

options.label: Abolish weekends
options.result_text: Calendars enter mourning. Payroll smiles. Citizens invent casual Mondays.

options.label: Half-day Saturday only
options.result_text: Saturday mornings become invoices. Brunch relocates to guilt.

options.label: Keep weekends
options.result_text: Weekends survive. Penny files a lament titled Lost Sundays.

authoring.visual_hook: tired citizens under mourning calendars
authoring.writer_notes: REWRITE from standalone; compromise half-day path required.

proposal: Your weekend policy left citizens horizontal. Nap lines form at noon. Productivity invented a limp.

options.label: Acknowledge the burnout
options.result_text: Olga names the nap lines. Penny calls them unscheduled audits.

options.label: Call it national hustle
options.result_text: Posters praise exhaustion. Citizens sleep through them.

authoring.visual_hook: noon nap lines after weekend cuts
authoring.writer_notes: Callback to abolish or half-day path.

proposal: Form W-7 asks whether weekends return, stay abolished, or lock your compromise. Attach nap-line attendance.

options.label: Restore full weekends
options.result_text: Sundays return. Penny files Lost Productivity with a soft stamp.

options.label: Keep weekends abolished
options.result_text: Calendars stay in mourning. Payroll remains cheerfully cruel.

options.label: Lock civic rest coupons
options.result_text: Rest becomes a stamped coupon. Brunch files Form Brunch-1.

authoring.visual_hook: mourning calendars vs rest coupons
authoring.writer_notes: State-dependent compromise branch required.

proposal: Policy needs templates. Found a State Meme Department. Citizens will share governance whether they mean to or not.

options.label: Found the meme ministry
options.result_text: Templates get letterhead. Luna sparkles. Clerks request caption stamps.

options.label: Pilot a caption desk only
options.result_text: One desk captions civic notices. Virality remains a rumor.

options.label: Refuse state memes
options.result_text: Policy stays in paragraphs. Luna schedules a lonely smile segment.

authoring.visual_hook: letterheaded meme ministry desk
authoring.writer_notes: Pool on full ministry; soft resolution on pilot.

proposal: Your State Meme Department went viral. Captions outrun laws. Form M-1 asks whether this is propaganda success or governance failure.

options.label: Call virality policy success
options.result_text: Luna airs 'Template Triumph.' Forms still wait unsigned.

options.label: Force templates through Form M-1
options.result_text: Every joke needs a stamp. Virality slows. Governance limps forward.

authoring.visual_hook: walls of stamped viral templates
authoring.writer_notes: Propaganda effectiveness vs governance cost branch.

proposal: Decide the meme desk's fate. Institutionalize the templates — or shut the department before captions pass a budget.

options.label: Make memes permanent policy
options.result_text: The ministry gets a permanent stamp drawer. Luna never blinks.

options.label: Shut the meme department
options.result_text: Templates archive themselves. Policy returns to dull sentences.

authoring.visual_hook: locked meme policy vault
authoring.writer_notes: Completes ministry or pilot path.

proposal: Storms poll poorly. Censor weather maps that show rain. Umbrellas are unofficial speculation.

options.label: Censor storm reporting
options.result_text: Maps stay sunny. Citizens leave umbrellas at home on principle.

options.label: Allow honest storm maps
options.result_text: Rain is named rain. Luna schedules a cheerful puddle segment.

authoring.visual_hook: blacked-out storm icons on civic maps
authoring.writer_notes: Distinct from weather_optimism_bulletin; soft credibility follow-up.

proposal: Your censored storm maps met real rain. Umbrella lines panic. Broadcast credibility drips onto the sidewalk.

options.label: Insist the rain is optional
options.result_text: Anchors smile through puddles. Trust evaporates faster than fog.

options.label: Restore storm maps quietly
options.result_text: Umbrellas return. Luna calls it a formatting update.

options.label: Issue emergency umbrella amnesty
options.result_text: Umbrellas become civic infrastructure. Forecasts stay optimistic on air.

authoring.visual_hook: umbrella panic lines under sunny bulletins
authoring.writer_notes: Affects order/credibility/behavior, not happiness-only.

proposal: Launch a National Talent Show. The ruler judges live. Ratings invent themselves.

options.label: Launch the talent show
options.result_text: Spotlights heat the square. Elites book front-row loyalty.

options.label: Refuse live judging
options.result_text: The stage stays dark. Luna files a lonely audition tape.

authoring.visual_hook: state talent stage with ruler chair
authoring.writer_notes: Soft budget scandal follow-up is the government consequence.

proposal: Your National Talent Show ate the culture budget. Elites want VIP seats. The ledger wants an intermission.

options.label: Bail out the production
options.result_text: Spotlights stay on. The treasury learns jazz hands.

options.label: Sell elite sponsor seats
options.result_text: Front rows become invoices. Applause arrives pre-purchased.

options.label: Cancel the talent show
options.result_text: Curtains drop mid-note. The culture line item exhales.

authoring.visual_hook: budget charts taped to stage lights
authoring.writer_notes: Meaningful budget/government consequence, not ratings fluff.

proposal: Silence reads as disloyalty on camera. Mandate applause quotas. Clap meters will smile for you.

options.label: Enact applause quotas
options.result_text: Meters tick. Soft hands become civic infrastructure.

options.label: Keep applause voluntary
options.result_text: Clapping stays sincere and rare. Luna calls it authentic risk.

authoring.visual_hook: public applause meters on plazas
authoring.writer_notes: Distinct from compliment quota; hard Boom enforcement.

proposal: Your applause quotas need boots. Soft hands miss quota. Boom offers a clap patrol with gentle elbows.

options.label: Deploy the clap patrol
options.result_text: Boots keep rhythm. Applause becomes a marching order.

options.label: Fine quiet citizens only
options.result_text: Quiet moments cost coins. Meters chirp like guilt.

authoring.visual_hook: clap patrol boots beside meters
authoring.writer_notes: Escalating enforcement; soft adaptation next.

proposal: Citizens adapted to your applause quotas with clap tracks and silent hand waves. Ratings still demand noise.

options.label: Ban clap tracks
options.result_text: Hands blister on schedule. Luna airs 'Authentic Thunder.'

options.label: License official clap tracks
options.result_text: Applause becomes a product. Silence requires headphones.

options.label: End applause quotas
options.result_text: Meters power down. Clapping becomes optional again — briefly shocking.

authoring.visual_hook: silent clap tracks and empty meters
authoring.writer_notes: Public adaptation outcomes; completes enforcement path.

proposal: Pilot an artificial sun over the capital. Nights become optional. Preliminary brightness only — maybe.

options.label: Launch the pilot sun
options.result_text: A small sun hums above the square. Shadows look confused.

options.label: Try a civic desk-lamp grid first
options.result_text: Streetlamps get ambitious. Night stays mostly night.

options.label: Refuse the artificial sun
options.result_text: Maybe shrugs. The moon keeps its job.

authoring.visual_hook: tiny artificial sun over capital square
authoring.writer_notes: Pool on full pilot; soft resolution on desk-lamp path.

proposal: Your pilot sun grew. Midnight looks like lunch. Chickens filed a complaint. Brightness is still preliminary.

options.label: Increase brightness carefully
options.result_text: Noon arrives at midnight. Maybe calls it a promising maybe.

options.label: Dim to a safer glow
options.result_text: The sun blushes softer. Sleep returns in draft form.

authoring.visual_hook: noon lighting at midnight in the capital
authoring.writer_notes: Escalation from pilot; outcomes follow chosen brightness path.

proposal: Form S-1 asks whether the artificial sun becomes a costly success, a shut-down experiment, or a dangerous failure. Attach shadow inventory.

options.label: Declare costly success
options.result_text: The sun stays — expensive and official. Historians buy sunglasses.

options.label: Shut the sun down with forms
options.result_text: Form S-1 extinguishes daylight. Night clocks in on time.

options.label: Force max brightness
options.result_text: The sun hiccups. The grid screams. Maybe says this was not the preferred maybe.

authoring.visual_hook: forms stamped over a dimming artificial sun
authoring.writer_notes: Costly success ending flags; fail starts national_power_outage.
